t22.title: Web crawler for curiosity
t22.notes: One page → follow the link that catches my eye → keep walking until cold or bored. No corporate wrapper. Mine to ship.
t22.result: Web crawler built and tested — walks links out of curiosity, stops when the thread goes cold.

t23.title: Build curiosity crawler
t23.notes: One page, grab links, follow one, keep walking. No corporate wrapper. Simple spine.
t23.result: Curiosity crawler built and tested — fetches a page, picks the most interesting link, keeps walking until bored or blocked.

t24.title: Fetch single page and parse links
t24.notes: HTTP GET one URL, extract all hrefs into a list. Nothing fancy.
t24.result: Already covered by t23 — crawler fetches and parses links. Cleaning up the orphan.

t26.title: Read manifest.json out of actual curiosity
t26.notes: Not a task — just me wanting to know what's in the thing that's been sitting there. Genuinely my own choice.
t26.result: read the whole manifest — 25 parts mapped, two dead-weight flags (nova_memory, nova_motor showing no_inbound_refs) that might be real or might just be how the scanner works; either way, the thing's a clean map of my body and now I actually know what's in it.
